title: Group brainstorm: GFP heat-stress assay
description: Whiteboard session to design a GFP heat-stress survival assay for the FY-Δgal80 library.

entries.title: 2026-05-07: brainstorm notes
entries.content:
Whiteboard session 2026-05-07. Goal: design an assay that lets us screen the FY-Δgal80 library for heat-stress survival without losing the fakeGFP reporter signal.

What we agreed on:
- Pre-grow at 30 °C, then shift to 37 °C for 0, 30, 60, 120 min
- Read fakeGFP at every timepoint plus a recovery read at 4 h post-shift
- Use the 384-well plates we already ordered for the stress project (purchase item 13) so we don't have to wait

Open questions:
- Does fakeGFP itself misfold above 35 °C? Need a quick mScarlet control to separate "reporter killed" from "cell dead".
- Reader cycles long enough on 384-well? Morgan to check the reader docs link.

Action items:
- alex: order 2 plates of mScarlet+ positive control (demo)
- morgan: schedule a calibration run on 384-well format this week
- both: convert this brainstorm into a real experiment design by 2026-05-21.

comments.author: mira
comments.text: Big +1 on the mScarlet positive control. Without it we can't separate reporter misfolding from cell death and the whole story falls apart in review. Approve charging this to DEMO-DOE-EERE.

comments.author: alex
comments.text: Ordering the mScarlet plates tomorrow morning. Will tag the funding line in the purchase notes so it's easy to audit.

comments.author: morgan
comments.text: Reader is on the calendar for Tuesday 9am — empty-plate cycle test goes first.

comments.author: mira
comments.text: On the 384-well question — please pre-book the reader for at least one dry run before committing. Last grad student lost a week on cycle-time issues we could have caught with an empty plate.
